page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-192 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 192 vCPUs mapped to 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 720 GB of RAM, providing 3.75 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the top tier for multi-core processing, including stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark CPU benchmarks. It exhibits strong capabilities in extended instruction execution, cryptography, and physics simulations, aided by hardware support for Advanced Vector Extensions and Advanced Matrix Extensions. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached read-only speeds and large-block read/write operations scoring in the top 10%. However, single-core performance and Passmark memory scores remain in the average tier.

The instance demonstrates high efficiency for parallelized workloads, achieving top-tier results in Redis SET operations, database operations, and static web serving throughput. It also performs exceptionally well in CPU-based LLM inference, particularly for prompt processing across small, medium, and large models. Given its high core density and memory bandwidth, the c4-standard-192 is highly suited for large-scale software compilation, database management, high-throughput web serving, and parallel scientific computing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-192 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 192 vCPUs and 720 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture with 96 physical cores and hyper-threading, it delivers top-tier multi-core performance, particularly in cryptography, physics simulations, and software compilation. The processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AMX, which accelerate matrix and vector operations. While single-core performance is average, its multi-threaded capabilities and memory bandwidth rank in the top 10% of benchmarks. This instance does not bundle local storage or GPUs. It is highly efficient for parallelized workloads, making it well-suited for database operations, high-throughput static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-192 features 192 vCPUs, 720 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for databases, compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-192 is a 192-vCPU server with 720 GB RAM, optimized for high-throughput multi-threaded workloads and databases.
tagline: A high-core-density compute instance delivering top-tier multi-threaded performance and advanced matrix acceleration for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs and 720 GB RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8581C processors.
Top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth in benchmark testing.
Hardware-accelerated AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets for vector and matrix math.
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and static web serving.
Suited for CPU-based LLM inference and highly parallelized enterprise workloads.
